# Driver Location Streaming

> Real-time GPS location updates from drivers, persisted as position history and broadcast to subscribers for live map tracking.

**Category:** Integration · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** real-time · gps · location · tracking · broadcast · geospatial

## What this does

Real-time GPS location updates from drivers, persisted as position history and broadcast to subscribers for live map tracking.

Specifies 4 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **driver_id** *(text, required)* — Identifier of the driver whose location is being updated.
- **latitude** *(number, required)* — WGS-84 decimal latitude.
- **longitude** *(number, required)* — WGS-84 decimal longitude.
- **altitude** *(number, optional)* — Altitude above sea level in meters.
- **heading** *(number, optional)* — Bearing in degrees from north (0-360).
- **speed** *(number, optional)* — Current speed in meters per second.
- **order_id** *(text, optional)* — Active order this position update is associated with.
- **destination_id** *(text, optional)* — Current waypoint/destination the driver is heading toward.

## What must be true

- **rule_01:** The driver app sends location updates on a regular interval (typically every few seconds while active).
- **rule_02:** Each update overwrites the driver's current location in the driver record.
- **rule_03:** A new position history record is only created if the driver has moved more than 50 meters from the last recorded position, or if no prior position exists.
- **rule_04:** Every location update triggers a broadcast event to the channel keyed on the driver's public identifier.
- **rule_05:** Subscribers listen on a per-driver channel to receive real-time location pushes.
- **rule_06:** Reverse geocoding to refresh city and country is throttled — only runs if 10+ minutes have elapsed since last geocode or if city/country are empty.
- **rule_07:** Location updates include the current active order and destination waypoint when available, enabling route tracking.

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Location Updated** — when driver sends a location update with latitude and longitude; driver record exists, then All subscribers on the driver's channel receive the new position in real time.
- **Position History Recorded** — when driver has moved more than 50 meters from last recorded position OR no prior position exists; location update received, then A persistent trail of driver positions is recorded for replay and audit.
- **Geocode Refreshed** — when 10 or more minutes have passed since last geocode, OR city or country fields are empty, then Driver's city and country are kept current for zone and routing logic.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Driver Not Found** — when provided driver_id does not match any driver record, then Update is rejected with an error response. *(error: `DRIVER_NOT_FOUND`)*

## Errors it can return

- `DRIVER_NOT_FOUND` — Driver resource not found.
- `LOCATION_UPDATE_INVALID` — Invalid coordinates provided for location update.

## Connects to

- **ride-request-lifecycle** *(recommended)* — Location updates are linked to the active order for trip tracking.
- **driver-app-flow** *(required)* — The driver app is the source of location updates.
- **eta-calculation** *(recommended)* — ETA is recalculated using updated driver position.
- **service-zones** *(optional)* — Zone entry/exit events can be derived from location changes.
